title: LHC micro black holes: is the collider safe?
question: Could high-energy collisions at the LHC produce a microscopic black hole that endangers Earth?

sources.title: Independent re-derivation of LHC black-hole safety bounds from 2024 neutron-star data

passages.verbatimText: Updated neutron-star cooling observations further constrain the accretion rate of any hypothetical stable black hole, independently reinforcing the conclusion that LHC collisions are safe.

claims.statement: 2024 neutron-star observations further constrain stable black-hole accretion, independently reinforcing LHC safety.
LHC collisions pose no credible danger of producing a black hole that could threaten Earth.

inferences.warrant: An independent, tighter empirical bound on accretion reinforces the safety conclusion.

overlays.label: Concerned skeptic
overlays.description: Distrusts the unobserved Hawking-radiation premise and worries the empirical bound may not cover at-rest capture.

assessments.rationale: Revised downward: unconvinced the new neutron-star bound closes the at-rest capture gap.
